phrases.intent_en: Ask if there are any Canadiens tickets available
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'il reste des billets pour le match du Canadien ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess-KEEL rest day bee-YEH poor luh MAHSH doo kah-nad-YAN
phrases.cultural_note: The Canadiens (le Canadien) are religion in Montréal. Locals say 'le Canadien' (singular) when referring to the team. Games sell out fast.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where Section 100 seats are located
phrases.text_local: Les sièges de la section cent, c'est par où ?
phrases.pronunciation: lay see-YEZH duh lah sek-SYON SAH say par OO
phrases.cultural_note: Centre Bell holds over 21,000 fans for hockey. The lower bowl (sections 100s) is closest to the ice and most sought-after.

responses.english: On the lower level, that way.

phrases.intent_en: Order a poutine and a beer at the concession
phrases.text_local: Une poutine et une bière, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: OON poo-TEEN ay OON bee-AIR seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Poutine — fries with cheese curds and gravy — is the quintessential Québec comfort food and a staple at Canadiens games.

responses.english: The poutine, ten dollars. The beer, nine.
responses.phonetic: lah poo-TEEN DEE doh-LAR lah BYAIR NUHF
responses.local: La poutine, dix dollars. La bière, neuf.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the team merchandise store is
phrases.text_local: La boutique officielle du Canadien, c'est où ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah boo-TEEK oh-fee-see-EL doo kah-nad-YAN say OO
phrases.cultural_note: The official Canadiens store sells jerseys, caps, and memorabilia. The CH (Club de Hockey) logo is one of sport's most iconic.

responses.english: On the ground floor, near the main entrance.
responses.phonetic: oh ray-duh-shoh-SAY pray duh lahn-TRAY prahn-see-PAHL
responses.local: Au rez-de-chaussée, près de l'entrée principale.

phrases.intent_en: Cheer with the crowd during the game
phrases.cultural_note: 'Lâchez pas' (don't give up / keep it up) is a quintessential Québécois rallying cry. Using it will earn instant approval from locals.
